name: INNER JOIN with table alias (3 rows: alice×2, bob×1)
command: SELECT u.name, o.amount FROM users u INNER JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id

name: LEFT JOIN preserves unmatched (4 rows: carol → None)
command: SELECT u.name, o.amount FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id

name: JOIN ... USING (id) matches by column name (3 rows)
command: SELECT u.name, t.tag FROM users u JOIN tags t USING (id)

name: Without index → NestedLoopJoin
command: EXPLAIN SELECT p.name, s.qty FROM products p INNER JOIN sales s ON p.id = s.product_id
stdout:
LogicalPlan
└── Project(table=products, cols=[name, qty])
    └── NestedLoopJoin(table=products, on=BinaryOp(op='=', left=ColumnRef(name='id', table='p'), right=ColumnRef(name='product_id', table='s')))
        ├── SeqScan(table=products)
        └── SeqScan(table=sales)
PhysicalPlan
└── Project(table=products, cols=[name, qty])
    └── NestedLoopJoin(table=products, on=BinaryOp(op='=', left=ColumnRef(name='id', table='p'), right=ColumnRef(name='product_id', table='s')))
        ├── SeqScan(table=products)
        └── SeqScan(table=sales)

name: With index → IndexedNestedLoopJoin
command: EXPLAIN SELECT p.name, s.qty FROM products p INNER JOIN sales s ON p.id = s.product_id
stdout:
LogicalPlan
└── Project(table=products, cols=[name, qty])
    └── IndexedNestedLoopJoin(table=products, on=BinaryOp(op='=', left=ColumnRef(name='id', table='p'), right=ColumnRef(name='product_id', table='s')))
        ├── SeqScan(table=products)
        └── SeqScan(table=sales)
PhysicalPlan
└── Project(table=products, cols=[name, qty])
    └── IndexedNestedLoopJoin(table=products, on=BinaryOp(op='=', left=ColumnRef(name='id', table='p'), right=ColumnRef(name='product_id', table='s')))
        ├── SeqScan(table=products)
        └── SeqScan(table=sales)

name: Indexed join still returns 3 rows (apple×2, banana×1)
command: SELECT p.name, s.qty FROM products p INNER JOIN sales s ON p.id = s.product_id

name: 5 readers concurrent (sum≈283ms vs total≈61ms)
command: 5× thread: rwlock.read() → SELECT COUNT(*) FROM kv

name: Writer blocks while reader holds lock (~100ms wait)
command: thread A: rwlock.read() + sleep(100ms); thread B: rwlock.write(timeout=500ms)
stdout: writer waited [100]ms for reader to release

name: READ COMMITTED — concurrent tx observe consistent snapshot
command: writer tx UPDATE 0→100 (holds 100ms); reader tx SELECT (during writer tx)

name: First process acquires ProcessLock (main proc, blocking)
command: ProcessLock(fp) → with-block

name: Child process blocked (timeout exceeded — lock contention confirmed)
command: subprocess with 2s timeout (should NOT return early)

name: After parent release, re-acquire is instant (0ms)
command: ProcessLock(fp2) immediately after release

name: one-shot SELECT prints column headers + all 3 rows
command: python -m tinydb --db X -c 'SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY id'
stdout:
id name  age
1  alice 30 
2  bob   25 
3  carol 35

name: one-shot INSERT returns affected row count
command: python -m tinydb --db X -c 'INSERT INTO users VALUES (4, "dave", 40)'

name: .explain prints LogicalPlan + PhysicalPlan tree
command: .explain SELECT a.x, b.val FROM a INNER JOIN b ON a.x = b.x
stdout:
LogicalPlan
└── Project(table=a, cols=[x, val])
    └── IndexedNestedLoopJoin(table=a, on=BinaryOp(op='=', left=ColumnRef(name='x', table='a'), right=ColumnRef(name='x', table='b')))
        ├── SeqScan(table=a)
        └── SeqScan(table=b)
PhysicalPlan
└── Project(table=a, cols=[x, val])
    └── IndexedNestedLoopJoin(table=a, on=BinaryOp(op='=', left=ColumnRef(name='x', table='a'), right=ColumnRef(name='x', table='b')))
        ├── SeqScan(table=a)
        └── SeqScan(table=b)

name: .tables lists all user tables (alpha + beta)
command: .tables (via list_tables())

name: JOIN emp+dept ordered by salary desc (3 rows, bob/Eng/120 first)
command: SELECT e.emp_name, d.dept_name, e.salary FROM emp e JOIN dept d ON e.dept_id = d.id ORDER BY e.salary DESC

name: UPDATE then re-query (bob now 150, alice still 100)
command: UPDATE emp SET salary=150 WHERE id=2; SELECT ... WHERE dept_id=10 ORDER BY salary DESC

name: v0.1 SELECT WHERE + ORDER BY still works (2 rows)
command: SELECT * FROM users WHERE age > 20 ORDER BY age ASC

name: v0.1 aggregate COUNT/AVG still works
command: SELECT COUNT(*), AVG(age) FROM users
